- Left comments on PRs and in Linear
Here's a summary of your Slack activity today:
#dev — debugging & troubleshooting
Worked through a payment intent / "Failed to create give intent" error in staging, traced it to an IP address check (ipinfo.io) bombing out — confirmed later in the day
Investigated a mobile giving minimum amount bug (testflight weirdness vs. prod issue) — walked someone through a diagnostic to isolate whether it was testflight-specific
Noted no major outage from what you saw
#dev — AI + code review discussion (substantial thread)
Kicked off or contributed heavily to a conversation about human accountability in AI-assisted PRs
Key points you made:
AI is diagnosing, planning, and building without enough human input before tagging reviewers
The social contract that "author understands what they're showing" has broken down with AI authorship
Suggested requiring a Loom walkthrough on AI-authored PRs (screen share, talk through code, demo it)
Raised two specific gaps: who formulates the plan, and who validates the PR actually addresses the issue
Shared a concrete example where AI misidentified a Platforms bug as a Web bug, pushed a fix, closed the Linear issue — and the PR description used language no human on the team had ever used
